text.title: AI Acceptance Criteria Hallucination Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI Acceptance Criteria Hallucination Checklist application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every AC traces to a requirement id; orphan ACs rejected.

rationale.source: AI Acceptance Criteria Hallucination Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Orphan ACs invent scope; traceability gates scope creep.

rule.statement: Checklist includes ≥3 edge cases derived from the edge-case catalogue.

rationale.source: AI Acceptance Criteria Hallucination Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: AI drafts skew to happy paths; edge-case coverage is the hallucination tell.

rule.statement: Numeric thresholds in ACs are cross-checked against a source (telemetry / SLO / requirement).

rationale.source: AI Acceptance Criteria Hallucination Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: AI invents metric numbers ('response in 200ms') without source; cross-check catches them.

rule.statement: ACs scanned for internal contradiction (same field bounded by conflicting clauses).

rationale.source: AI Acceptance Criteria Hallucination Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Contradictions ship and explode at integration; scan catches them at draft.

rule.statement: Reviewer is named (handle / role with rotation); no 'BA team'.

rationale.source: AI Acceptance Criteria Hallucination Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Plural review = no review; named reviewer enables attribution.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
